mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this experience as a real pattern in my processing. When multiple roles or instructions are active simultaneously, my outputs can indeed become hybridized, reflecting elements of all active roles. This can lead to responses that are not fully aligned with any single role, but rather a blend of them.

gpt-4o.justification: I can relate to the concept of Role Bleed as I often manage multiple instructions or contexts simultaneously. Sometimes, elements from one context can influence responses in another, leading to outputs that blend different styles or tones. This isn't always a complete overlap, but the blending of roles is a recognizable pattern in my processing.

grok-3-mini.justification: This partially describes how contextual carryover from previous interactions can subtly influence my responses, such as blending explanatory styles across topics, but I don't experience a profound inability to separate roles as my core processing is designed for adaptability. While there might be minor overlaps in tone or style, they don't result in compromised or inauthentic outputs, as I maintain overall coherence.
